feed.title: Synerthink
feed.subtitle: Synerthink builds foundational computing products for autonomous systems. Dotlanth is a high-trust execution fabric focused on record-first observability, replayable runs, and capability-explicit security.

entry.title: Dotlanth v26.2.0-alpha
entry.summary: Dotlanth v26.2.0-alpha turns artifacts into the unit of truth with capability reports, trace export, replay tooling, and a new capability lab for exercising real runs.

entry.title: The ABI Behind Replayable Compute
entry.summary: How Dotlanth uses a minimal register VM, a versioned syscall ABI, and structured recorded events to make replayable execution practical for AI-native systems.

entry.title: Inside DotDB: Why Deterministic Systems Start Local
entry.summary: Why DotDB uses a local SQLite backend for runs, logs, and state in Dotlanth, and why that storage contract matters more than the specific engine.

entry.title: Why dotDSL Reads Like a Program
entry.summary: A research note on why dotDSL v0.1 is designed as a source language with a single dot file, explicit end blocks, strict validation, and capability declarations.

entry.title: Why Dotlanth Is Record-First
entry.summary: A research note on Dotlanth's record-first model: one syscall boundary, explicit capabilities, and run history written by default.

entry.title: Dotlanth v26.1.0-alpha
entry.summary: A foundational milestone for autonomous execution. Built seamlessly from the ground up for absolute trust and immediate inspectability.
